posts.name: Ilkka |_ |_| mme
posts.cooked:
I don’t mind that almost no one is talking about this producer as I wouldn’t want the prices to go up but if you haven’t tried their wines you need to do yourself a favor and get some. I suppose this red is the easiest one to appreciate with the white and the rosé being more acquired tastes. I’ve been buying this since the 2013 vintage and opening this bottle served as a good reminder to keep going. Missus likes Italian reds the most but absolutely loves this one, which is a plus.

  • 2015 Château Simone Palette - France, Provence, Palette (3.12.2021)
    Popped and poured, enjoyed over three hours. Smoky and dusty on the nose with a very savory character. Wonderful dark cherry fruit along with notes of herbs and tobacco and a slight hint of VA. On the palate medium bodied with terrific ripeness yet it comes off as savory and fresh. Good focus and lift with extremely appropriate tannins that provide structure and grip but no hard edges. Generously fruited with some warmth, spice and bitter dark chocolate, the perfect taste profile for the winter. A bit chewy with terrific length. Amazingly approachable and satisfying despite no decanting.

Posted from CellarTracker

Posted from CellarTracker

I’m a big fan, too. Apparently there’s some restaurant in Provence that has magnums from the '40s on the list, but the friend that knows about it quite reasonably refuses to tell me where it is unless he’s present to drink the bottles with me.

posts.name: Tvrtko Cernos
posts.cooked:
Missus likes Italian reds the most

Clever lady, obviously.

As for Simone: I normally like the red very much and, while I am not entirely sold on the rose’, I absolutely adore the white in most vintages.

posts.name: LasseK
posts.cooked:
I am not that much into the red… had the 2012 a few months back, didn’t do much for me.

But the white is absolutely amazing with some age. Had the 2001 back in late 2017. One of the best whites i have tasted.

posts.name: P@u1_M3nk3s
posts.cooked: I think red, white and pink are all age-worthy, though I seem to enjoy the rose best at around age 5 or 6.

posts.name: Al Osterheld
posts.cooked:
Love all the wines, red, white, and pink.

-Al

posts.name: Michael Baryla
posts.cooked: I love the Simones. I just picked up a couple cases from distribution. There is still plenty of stock going back almost 10 years

If you one day end up there with your buddy I trust you’ll come here soon after to share the name and address with us

posts.name: Ilkka |_ |_| mme
posts.cooked:
Missus likes Italian reds the most

Clever lady, obviously.

As for Simone: I normally like the red very much and, while I am not entirely sold on the rose’, I absolutely adore the white in most vintages.

She is. I often find myself wanting to like a wine and looking for redeeming qualities but there is none of that with her.

I’ve tried the rosé only once and it needed a lot of air to get going but I felt like it would for sure develop into something great when given enough time in the cellar. I need to find out whether I’m right or not.

posts.name: Ilkka |_ |_| mme
posts.cooked:
I am not that much into the red… had the 2012 a few months back, didn’t do much for me.

But the white is absolutely amazing with some age. Had the 2001 back in late 2017. One of the best whites i have tasted.

Well color me surprised. I know you like Rhône Syrahs a lot, how about the South? I kind of feel like there is some Southern Rhône and some Bandol in this one but then there are other sides to it as well with the dusty qualities taking me to Tuscany just a bit.

posts.name: LasseK
posts.cooked:
I am not that much into the red… had the 2012 a few months back, didn’t do much for me.

But the white is absolutely amazing with some age. Had the 2001 back in late 2017. One of the best whites i have tasted.

Well color me surprised. I know you like Rhône Syrahs a lot, how about the South? I kind of feel like there is some Southern Rhône and some Bandol in this one but then there are other sides to it as well with the dusty qualities taking me to Tuscany just a bit.

I was surprised too… tasted it all young and fresh a few years back. I was not blown away, but it sure had some nice Syrah traits, so i bought one and put it away. But the bottle i had recently had developed in another direction. I did not take any notes that night, but i had it next to a 2014 Halcon Alturas Syrah. And they were very far from each other.
